title: --userns=keepid does not work when launching podman containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

<!--
we have setup podman to run in rootless mode but --userns=keepid does not work in scenario when homedir of user is on NFS fileshare and same home dir is mounted on to the container. I am using podman, so commands with docker are actually podman commands.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
/usr/bin/docker run -v /opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf:/opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf -v /u/asmalvan/:/u/asmalvan -v /tmp:/tmp -w /u/asmalvan --userns=keep-id -it ubuntu bash
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: chdir to cwd ("/u/asmalvan") set in config.json failed: permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
We were expecting that user name space should be maintained inside the container. I am user root inside the container but I want to be a user who has spawned the container which in this case is user asmalvan. The use case is helpful in launching podman container in schedulers like LSF which help with scheduling and execution of HPC workloads
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version
podman version 1.9.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
baremetal machine

comments.body:
The `--userns=keep-id` flag does what you are describing - it sets userns mappings so the user that launched the container will retain their UID in the container, and defaults the container to launch as that user. I'm quite confident this bit is working, so it's most likely an environment problem.

What are the permissions of the folder you've mounted in as the workdir, `/u/asmavlan`? Do you have SELinux enabled, and if so are there any AVCs after the container launches?

Yes this is either SELinux or Unix permissions not allowing the user to enter /u/asmavlan.

ls -lZ /u/asmavlan
ls -la /u

@rhatdan 
```
$ ls -la /u
total 24
drwxr-xr-x   6 root     root     4096 Mar 25 12:55 .
drwxr-xr-x. 21 root     root     4096 Apr 14 23:01 ..
drwx------   7 asmalvan users    4096 May  5 16:03 asmalvan

$ ls -la /u/asmalvan/
total 124
drwx------ 7 asmalvan users  4096 May  5 16:03 .
drwxr-xr-x 6 root     root   4096 Mar 25 12:55 ..
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users 29588 May  5 16:03 .bash_history
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users    18 Mar 24 13:37 .bash_logout
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users   141 Mar 24 13:37 .bash_profile
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users   376 Mar 24 13:37 .bashrc
drwx------ 3 asmalvan users  4096 Mar 29 15:41 .config
drwx------ 3 asmalvan users  4096 Mar 29 15:08 .local
-rw-r--r-- 1 root     root  23851 Apr  6 12:31 lsb.applications.ccc3
drwx------ 2 asmalvan users  4096 May  5 12:29 .lsbatch
-rw-r--r-- 1 root     root   3003 Apr  6 12:32 lsb.params.ccc3
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users     7 Apr  5 12:28 .python_history
-rw-r--r-- 1 asmalvan users  4082 Apr 14 23:26 setup_lsf_podman.sh
drwx------ 2 asmalvan users  4096 Apr  1 14:59 .ssh
drwxr-xr-x 2 asmalvan users  4096 Apr 18 19:38 .vim
-rw------- 1 asmalvan users 13634 May  4 12:11 .viminfo

$ ls -lZ /u/asmalvan/
total 36
-rw-r--r-- 1 root     root  ? 23851 Apr  6 12:31 lsb.applications.ccc3
-rw-r--r-- 1 root     root  ?  3003 Apr  6 12:32 lsb.params.ccc3
-rw-r--r-- 1 asmalvan users ?  4082 Apr 14 23:26 setup_lsf_podman.sh

$ getenforce
Disabled


```


Can you please share how to fix this SElinux issue?

> The `--userns=keep-id` flag does what you are describing - it sets userns mappings so the user that launched the container will retain their UID in the container, and defaults the container to launch as that user. I'm quite confident this bit is working, so it's most likely an environment problem.
> 
> What are the permissions of the folder you've mounted in as the workdir, `/u/asmavlan`? Do you have SELinux enabled, and if so are there any AVCs after the container launches?

Hi @mheon  This command works without --userns=keepid, but I am root inside the container not user asmalvan:

$ /usr/bin/docker run -v /opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf:/opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf -v /u/asmalvan/:/u/asmalvan -v /tmp:/tmp  -w /u/asmalvan -it ubuntu 

For my use case, I want to be user `asmalvan` inside the container once it is launched.

Hm. I note that you're on Podman 1.9.3 - that's probably the cause. RHEL 8.3.1 has 2.2.1, which should be recent enough to do what you want (or, even better, go to the newly-released 8.4.0 with Podman 3.0.1)

Thanks @mheon for confirming that it is possible with podman 2.2.1, it will help us run HPC workloads with podman at scale. 
So at this point I assume there is no way this could be enabled with podman 1.9.3, upgrading to a new OS and then making changes to the scheduler will be an arduous task for a cluster with thousands of machines.

If it is not possible to upgrade, you can try doing `--user UID:GID` (substituting the UID and GID with the UID/GID of the logged-in user). However, I strongly encourage you to upgrade - 1.9.3 is out of support now.

@mheon  thanks, below commands do not work:

```
$ /usr/bin/docker run -v /opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf:/opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf -v /u/asmalvan/:/u/asmalvan -v /tmp:/tmp  --user 599523:100 --userns=keep-id  -w /u/asmalvan -it ubuntu
Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: chdir to cwd ("/u/asmalvan") set in config.json failed: permission denied: OCI runtime permission denied error
[asmalvan@ccc3-login-01 ~]$ /usr/bin/docker run -v /opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf:/opt/ibm/lsfsuite/lsf -v /u/asmalvan/:/u/asmalvan -v /tmp:/tmp  --user 599523:100 -w /u/asmalvan -it ubuntu
Error: container_linux.go:370: starting container process caused: setup user: invalid argument: OCI runtime error

```

/u/asmalvan is homedir on NFS
Ah. NFS is the problem, then. We do not support multiple UIDs and GIDs in containers when Podman storage is on NFS yet (@giuseppe has plans to include support in fuse-overlayfs in the future, AFAIK).
@mheon  so it's not supported in podmanv2 or just on podmanv1.9?

Not supported in any Podman release at present. The NFS protocol does not understand user namespaces and ID mapping, which are required for using more than one UID/GID in a rootless container. We have theories on how we can work around this but none of them have been implemented yet.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#14-rootless-podman-build-fails-eperm-on-nfs

Since this is a known issue, and fixes will come when we have kernels in place that support NFS with Xattrs, I am going to close. Our plan is to have some level of support in RHEL8.5 release in the fall.
